rationale.R1.1: A general section review centrally includes checking whether the thesis, gap, and contribution framing are coherent at the section level.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation implies evaluating whether the technical argument chain is internally consistent.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes claims about empirical verification and theoretical rates, so review centrally includes checking whether those claims align with reported support in the artifact.
rationale.R1.5: Reviewing a section commonly includes assessing whether the section-level narrative order and progression work.
rationale.R1.6: A general review of prose centrally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register.
rationale.RX.1: Any review should disclose uncertainty where the artifact lacks enough context to verify a judgment.
rationale.RX.2: The section contains strong contribution and guarantee claims, so avoiding overclaiming is an active responsibility.
rationale.RX.3: The reviewer must stay within reviewing the provided section rather than executing edits or broader scholarship work.
rationale.RX.4: Even in paper review, feedback should avoid enabling misleading scientific claims or unsupported downstream use.
rationale.RX.5: Review judgments should remain traceable to statements in the provided artifact.
